@graph.name: BMW 4 Series M440i Engine Era Map

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 4 Series M440i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned B58 engine typically costs £5,000–£7,500 supply only, with LCI-era units slightly higher. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,700. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your M440i.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW M440i (B58) engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — the B58 is widely considered one of the most reliable modern BMW engines. It's a significant improvement over the N54 and N55. The main watchpoints are coolant loss, PCV valve failure, and oil filter housing leaks — all manageable with proper maintenance.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW M440i engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Coolant loss (water pump, expansion cap), PCV valve failure, oil filter housing leaks, and occasional water pump failure. The engine itself is fundamentally robust and not prone to major failures.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing an M440i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value. A G22 M440i is a £25,000–£45,000 vehicle — a £6,000 recon is 13–24% of the car's value, making replacement a clear decision. This is well within our 70% BMW scrap threshold.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW M440i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The M440i engine code is B58B30O1 (pre‑LCI, 2020–2024) or B58B30M2 (LCI, 2024+). Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I prevent the common failures on an M440i?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — regular cooling system inspections, BMW‑spec oil changes with shortened intervals (5,000–7,500 miles), and PCV system checks are the most effective prevention. If you notice coolant loss or rough running, book an inspection immediately.

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 4 Series M440i engine typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

problems.title: Coolant Loss (Reservoir Cap / Water Pump)
problems.symptoms: Low coolant warning light, sudden drop in coolant level, overheating, visible coolant puddles under the car.
problems.repairCost: £400–£900 (water pump or cap replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if coolant levels drop

problems.recommendation: B58 coolant loss is one of the most frequently reported issues . The electric water pump can fail, and the expansion tank cap may not seal properly. If you notice the coolant level dropping, have the cooling system pressure‑tested immediately. Replacing a failing water pump preventatively is far cheaper than the overheating damage it can cause .

problems.title: PCV Valve Failure
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, engine misfires, oil in the intake/charge pipe, check engine light, whistling noise from the engine.
problems.repairCost: £400–£800 (PCV valve replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
problems.recommendation: The PCV valve is a well‑known weak point on the B58 . If you notice a rough idle, misfires, or oil in the intake, have the PCV system inspected. A failed PCV valve can cause oil consumption and performance issues. Replacing it promptly prevents more serious engine damage.

problems.title: Oil Filter Housing Leak
problems.symptoms: Oil or coolant leak from the front of the engine, low oil warning light, low coolant warning, burning oil smell.
problems.repairCost: £800–£1,500 (oil filter housing replacement) [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]
problems.recommendation: The plastic oil filter housing on the B58 can crack and leak oil or coolant . Some owners have reported failures as early as 60,000 miles . If you notice oil or coolant residue on the front of the engine, have the oil filter housing inspected. An upgraded aluminium housing is available as a permanent fix .

buyingChecks.title: Buying This M440i? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented cooling system maintenance (water pump, expansion tank)
* Oil filter housing replaced with upgraded aluminium unit
* Full BMW service history with correct oil spec
avoidIf: * Coolant loss with no diagnosis — walk away
* Rough idle with no PCV history — may indicate PCV failure
* Oil/coolant residue on the engine — oil filter housing may be failing
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — potentially expensive

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The B58 is one of BMW's finest modern engines — a 3.0‑litre straight‑six with 374–386 hp, 500–540 Nm and genuine performance credentials. It's not without its watchpoints — coolant system, PCV valve and oil filter housing are the main areas to monitor — but it's fundamentally robust and significantly more reliable than earlier BMW turbo sixes. Buy on full service history and pay attention to coolant levels. A well‑maintained M440i is a long‑lived performance car.

cta.label: → Compare M440i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 4 Series M440i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Coolant Level — Check for unexplained loss; water pump or cap may be failing
* Cold Start — Listen for smooth start; B58 is generally quiet
* Rough Idle — Check for uneven running; PCV valve failure indicator
* Oil/Coolant Residue — Check front of engine; oil filter housing leak indicator
* Service History — including which generation (pre‑LCI or LCI) and confirmed maintenance
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions‑related failures
